summary: Gruber announces a change to Daring Fireball's Linked List permalink structure, moving from month-based URLs to individual per-entry permalinks. The new format is cleaner and more logical, following a date-and-slug pattern. Old URLs continue to work, honoring the principle that cool URIs don't change. He acknowledges the one downside: RSS feed readers may have marked previously read items as unread due to the URL change.
key_points: Linked List entries now have individual permalink URLs instead of month-based anchor links
Old URLs are preserved and still work, following W3C guidance that URIs should be stable
The new URL structure supports browsable hierarchical navigation by day and month
The change caused a one-time RSS inconvenience where read items may have been marked unread
money_quotes: The new URLs are better, and, in hindsight, are the way things always should have been.
You can also get listings by day and month by chopping the URL.
key_insight: Good URL design means each piece of content deserves its own clean, permanent, hackable address — and when you fix a mistake, you preserve the old paths.
